parts: Cincinalis tenera, also known as Indian cinnamon, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. Its medicinal parts are primarily the bark and roots, which are rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bark is often used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems, while the roots are used to treat fever and other inflammatory conditions.
